items.description: Numerals '83' written in small dark ink in the upper right corner.
items.scholarly_note: Foliation mark, likely added during post-medieval cataloguing or rebinding at the Danish Royal Library or by Árni Magnússon; standard for this codex.

items.description: Large decorated initial 'F' approximately five lines high with simple pen flourishes and leftward extension at the opening of the textual section.
items.scholarly_note: Standard Gothic bookhand initial marking the start of a stanza or section in Brot af Sigurðarkviðu; modest penwork typical of the main scribe's practice.

items.description: Large rounded initial (appearing as 'C') with elongated descender/tail extending downward several lines on the left side of the lower text block.
items.scholarly_note: Decorated initial marking a new section (Guðrúnarkviða I context); the long descender is a common decorative feature in this manuscript's mise-en-page.

items.description: Small isolated 'y'-shaped or curved stroke in dark ink below the final lines of text, separate from the main text block.
items.scholarly_note: Possible stray pen trial, ink test, or fragmentary letter; faint and ambiguous at current resolution. Matches the 'pending identification' note in project doodles catalog.

items.description: Irregular parchment loss and torn edge at the lower right corner, with text from the final lines partially affected or lost.
items.scholarly_note: Significant mechanical damage from handling, binding, or trimming; consistent with known edge wear on many folios of GKS 2365 4to.

items.description: Concentrated brownish discoloration and mottled staining in the lower right quadrant adjacent to the damaged corner.
items.scholarly_note: Age-related foxing combined with possible liquid damage or iron-gall ink bleed; typical of calf vellum in Icelandic manuscripts of this period.

items.description: Scattered brownish age stains, darker patches, and edge discoloration running along the right margin and into the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Common iron-gall and environmental staining; some patches coincide with areas of thinner parchment showing possible show-through.

items.description: Localized areas of surface abrasion and rubbed ink within the text block, particularly visible in lines flagged for repeated glyphs.
items.scholarly_note: Hair-side abrasions or post-production wear; may be responsible for some 'repeated glyph' appearances rather than true scribal errors.

items.description: Letters in multiple lines (approx. lines 4, 8, 12, 15, 17, 19, 24) appear darker, retraced, or slightly doubled in ink.
items.scholarly_note: Possible scribal self-corrections, hesitation, or later reinforcement; project QC flags these as repeated glyphs. No clear expunctions or interlinear insertions visible.

items.description: Faint reverse impressions and slight ink ghosting visible through the parchment in several areas.
items.scholarly_note: Offset ink or bleed-through from the verso, typical of thin, well-prepared Icelandic calf vellum and iron-gall ink.

scratches: Localized parchment surface abrasion in text block mid-right and lower areas causing ink loss or thickening
Linear handling scratches and edge wear on the damaged lower right corner
Minor rubbed spots coinciding with darker or repeated letter forms in the main text
damage_notes: Large irregular loss of parchment at lower right corner with torn and worn edge
Irregular trimmed or abraded page edges, especially right and bottom
General cockling, thinning, and age-related distortion of the vellum sheet
Scattered small dark specks (possible flyspeck, ink splatter, or corrosion products)
summary: Page 94 of Codex Regius (GKS 2365 4to) contains text from Brot af Sigurðarkviðu / Guðrúnarkviða I written in the main scribe's Gothic bookhand on well-prepared calf vellum. The surface is relatively clean of added marginalia, doodles, faces, or animals. The most prominent non-text features are two large decorated initials (F and C with descender), a foliation '83' in the upper right, a small stray 'y'-like mark in the lower margin, extensive age stains and foxing (especially lower right), and significant mechanical damage with parchment loss at the lower right corner. Minor text-block abrasions and possible letter retracings are present; no clear interlinear glosses, pen trials in margins, or figurative drawings are visible. Overall the page reflects typical wear for the codex with limited secondary annotation.
